title: Peppery Velvety Irish Shiitake Stew
description: A comforting, pepper‑laden Irish stew with silky shiitake mushrooms and a creamy finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and cook until they release moisture and start to brown, about 7-8 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, thyme, and rosemary, cooking for another 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the Irish brown ale, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot; let it reduce by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth, diced carrots, and celery; bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and let the stew simmer for 30 minutes, then uncover and continue cooking for another 15 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the heavy cream and butter, allowing the butter to melt fully, and simmer for an additional 5 minutes to achieve a velvety texture.
Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste; serve hot over a bed of crusty gluten‑free bread or with boiled potatoes.
